channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Southern California native paid off mortgage day before Eaton Fire consumed home
item.description: While those displaced by the Eaton and Palisades fires are still reeling from the loss of their homes and, in some cases, loved ones, many of them say they’re being victimized yet again by price-gouging landlords.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Steven Soderbergh, Questlove, Ron Howard and more pay tribute to David Lynch
item.description: Reactions to the death of David Lynch, the visionary filmmaker behind “Twin Peaks” and “Mulholland Drive,” whose death at 78 was announced Thursday. — “He’s one of those filmmakers who was influential but impossible to imitate.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Starship upper stage lost on seventh test flight, debris seen spewing in sky
item.description: Rocket debris from a SpaceX's Starship Super Heavy booster was spotted in the sky after a "rapid unscheduled disassembly.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Giuliani settles legal fight with former Georgia election workers and agrees to stop defaming them
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Rudy Giuliani reached a deal Thursday that lets the cash-strapped ex-New York City mayor keep his homes and belongings, including prized World Series rings, in exchange for unspecified compensation and a promise to never again speak ill of two former Georgia elections workers who won
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: PHOTOS: 'Home Alone' house in Illinois sells for $250K over asking price
item.description: A famous house featured in the iconic holiday film "Home Alone" sold this week for nearly $300,000 over its asking price.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, KRON4

item.title: Nazi flag-bearing man gets 8 years in prison for truck crashing near White House
item.description: A Missouri man who crashed a rental truck into barriers protecting the White House has been sentenced to eight years in prison.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Official portraits of Donald Trump, JD Vance are released
item.description: Not waiting until after his inauguration, on Thursday President-elect Trump gave the country a look at what he says are the official portraits of himself and Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: As Biden warns of an 'oligarchy,' Trump will be flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: The super-rich have long played a role in U.S. politics but have an unusually prominent spot in incoming President Donald Trump's new administration.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi And Ali Swenson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Unsolved Ohio: Father pushes for answers eight years after son's suspicious death
item.description: View NBC4’s previous coverage in the video player above. COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) – Eight years after Joey LaBute vanished from a Columbus bar and was later found dead, his father is revealing new information surrounding the case in hopes of finding answers. Joseph Labute Jr.
item.dc:creator: Ava Boldizar, NewsNation

item.title: College students charged in TikTok-inspired 'catch a predator' plot appear in court
item.description: WORCESTER, Mass. (AP) — Five Massachusetts college students appeared in court Thursday, accused of plotting to lure a man to their campus through a dating app and then seizing him as part of a “Catch a Predator” trend on TikTok.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about RedNote, a TikTok alternative
item.description: TikTok patrons are turning to a new platform in light of the nationwide ban set to shut down the app on Sunday. RedNote, a Chinese short-form video platform, is gaining ground as users look for an alternative to the social media company owned by the China's ByteDance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: LA authorities searching for at least 36 missing people as wildfire death count rises to 25
item.description: The death toll from the Los Angeles wildfires rose to 25 on Thursday, while at least 36 people remain missing, city and county authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: David Lynch, director of 'Twin Peaks,' 'Blue Velvet' and more, dies at 78, family says
item.description: David Lynch, the filmmaker and director behind such movies as “Eraserhead,” “Blue Velvet” and the TV series “Twin Peaks,” has died, his family announced in a message posted to his official Facebook page.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Banning cellphones in schools gains popularity in red and blue states
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Arkansas' Republican Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders and California's Democratic Gov.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jeff Bezos’ New Glenn rocket reaches orbit on first test flight
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — Blue Origin launched its massive new rocket on its first test flight Thursday, sending up a prototype satellite to orbit thousands of miles above Earth.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fetterman's approach to Trump stirs speculation
item.description: Sen. John Fetterman’s (D-Pa.) open-minded approach to President-elect Trump is fueling questions about his motives and vexing some of his fellow Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Small California town considers backing mass deportations
item.description: "The way the language was presented in the resolution, it suggests that people should be scared of immigrants because they are criminals," said Rios. "It would target them by other members of the community who believe immigrants are causing harm and need to be sanctioned by the city of El Cajon.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance's Ohio hometown struggles with how to recognize the incoming vice president
item.description: MIDDLETOWN, Ohio (AP) — It was a month after her son's election as vice president of the United States and JD Vance 's mother wondered why the city of his birth had yet to recognize him.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump adviser says president-elect is exploring options to 'preserve' TikTok
item.description: Trump’s pick for national security adviser, Florida Rep. Mike Waltz, said in an interview on Wednesday that the president-elect is exploring options to “preserve” TikTok.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man sought after 'So I raped you' Facebook message being extradited to US
item.description: French Appeal Court prosecutors said an American man accused of sexually assaulting a Pennsylvania college student in 2013 and later sending her a Facebook message that said, “So I raped you," was being extradited Thursday to the United States.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Biden's new executive order aims to shore up US cyber defenses
item.description: An executive order issued by President Joe Biden just days before he leaves office aims to shore up America's cyber defenses while making it easier to go after foreign countries that launch cyberattacks.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: American accused of assaulting a Pennsylvania student is extradited from France to the US
item.description: An American accused of sexually assaulting a Pennsylvania college student in 2013 and later sending her a Facebook message that said “So I raped you” has been extradited from France to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Nicolas Vaux-Montagny And Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Florida man attacks elderly woman, robs her of lottery winnings: surveillance footage
item.description: Deputies in Orange County, Florida, are investigating the robbery of an 83-year-old woman after a man, believed to be Diego Stalin Tavarez Fleury, stole her lottery winnings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sen. John Curtis issues statement in support of Pete Hegseth, Trump's nominee for defense secretary
item.description: The day after Pete Hegseth's confirmation hearing, Sen. John Curtis, R-Utah, released a statement in support of Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Prominent Puerto Rican civil rights leader José 'Cha Cha' Jiménez dies at 76
item.description: José “Cha Cha” Jiménez, a prominent civil rights and liberation movement figure and founder of the Young Lords in Chicago and co-founder of the Rainbow Coalition has died. He was 76. His sister, Daisy Rodríguez, said in a Facebook post that he died Friday morning. A cause of death was not given.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida bill would ban students in the US without legal permission from some public universities
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida state lawmaker has filed a bill that would ban some public colleges and universities from admitting immigrants who are in the country without legal permission. The proposal comes the day after Gov.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hegseth survives attacks from Democrats in testy Senate hearing
item.description: Pete Hegseth on Tuesday battled through a high-stakes Senate hearing in which Democrats sought to paint the Pentagon nominee as inexperienced, unprofessional and toxic due to allegations of sexual abuse and heavy drinking.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, Fox 5

item.title: Capital One sued by US watchdog, accused of cheating customers out of $2 billion
item.description: A U.S. watchdog is suing Capital One for allegedly misleading consumers about its offerings for high-interest savings accounts — and “cheating" customers out of more than $2 billion in lost interest payments as a result.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chemicals in sewage sludge fertilizer pose cancer risk, EPA says
item.description: Harmful chemicals in sewage sludge that is spread on pasture land as fertilizer are causing cancer, the Environmental Protection Agency said Tuesday. The risk is highest for people who regularly consume milk, beef and other products from farms where it is spread.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dementia cases expected to double by 2060: Study
item.description: Dementia cases in the U.S. are expected to double by 2060 when around 1 million Americans are projected to develop the memory-losing condition every year, according to a new study published Monday in the medical journal Nature Medicine.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: REAL ID enforcement may be phased, but deadline is the same: What to know
item.description: The REAL ID deadline is fast-approaching and officials want you to be prepared (spoiler: you may already be in compliance).
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: USDA documented insects and slime at Boar's Head plants, records show
item.description: Government inspectors documented unsanitary conditions at several Boar's Head deli meat plants, not just the factory that was shut down last year after a deadly outbreak of listeria poisoning, federal records show.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA AP Health Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hegseth grilled over comments on women serving in combat
item.description: The start to hearings for President-elect Donald Trump’s cabinet nominees kicked off on Tuesday with Pete Hegseth who is hoping to become the next secretary of defense.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WGN 9

item.title: Interactive maps show homes damaged, destroyed in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: The Los Angeles County Fire Department has provided maps of the thousands of structures, primarily homes, that were damaged or destroyed in the Palisades and Eaton wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: Princess Kate says her cancer is in remission
item.description: The Princess of Wales revealed Tuesday that her cancer is in remission after an emotional visit to the hospital where she received treatment last year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
